title: Finder capabilities (Finder → Matcher)
description: Self-description a finder publishes so the matcher knows which portals/fields/features it offers. Lets the matcher disable scorers that have no data (e.g. the relative scorer when `relatives` is not produced) and set honest user expectations. Served at GET /capabilities. The matcher treats every field as UNTRUSTED (published by a possibly third-party finder) and MUST escape all free-text (`finderVersion`, `portal.name`, `portal.regions[]`) before rendering it in the admin UI.

finderId.description: Stable identifier for this finder deployment (used to key the matcher's per-finder negative-result memory).

finderVersion.description: Human-readable version of the finder software. Capabilities are stable per finderVersion — the matcher may cache them and refetch on a change.

retentionSeconds.description: Minimum seconds a terminal (done/failed) job is retained before GET /jobs/{id} starts returning 404 (job_expired). The matcher MUST fetch results within this window.

schemaVersions.description: Contract major versions this finder supports.

noticeFields.description: Which best-effort notice fields this finder can populate. Only name/url/source/fetchedAt are guaranteed on every notice; everything else (including `death`, the answer) is best-effort and declared here so the matcher knows which scorers have data and whether death dates are delivered at all.

pagination.description: Searches beyond the first results page.

regionRouting.description: Uses a candidate's region hint to pick portals.

reverseMatch.description: Supports the future reverse mode (recent notices by region). Reserved; not required for v1.

id.description: Stable portal id, referenced by Notice.source and JobOptions.portals.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code the portal primarily serves. SHOULD be populated — the matcher's region routing has nothing to work with otherwise.
